prose:
**Oxygen frontier-orbital result** — Property: HOMO–LUMO gap from a frontier-orbitals calculation. Level of theory: DFT tier `standard`, explicitly `b3lyp/def2-tzvp` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP), PySCF. Geometry: fixed user-supplied XYZ file `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/oxygen/oxygen.xyz` with 2 atoms; no geometry optimization was performed by this task. Environment: gas phase (`solvent: null`). Charge 0, multiplicity 3. Density fitting was not requested. SCF converged: true, in 7 cycles. Engine integrity trustworthy verdict: true.

Headline result: HOMO–LUMO gap = **5.2880586 eV**.

Frontier orbital energies returned by chemkit: HOMO = -8.8739616 eV; LUMO = -3.5859031 eV. Total electronic energy = -150.38872 Hartree = -4092.2855 eV.

Warnings: no warnings were reported by the tool result.

Reproducibility: method used was chemkit `frontier-orbitals` with PySCF DFT, `b3lyp/def2-tzvp`, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 3. CLI invocation returned by the engine: `/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/oxygen/oxygen.xyz --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 3 --stdout path --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmphao48rwh/live.json`. The returned JSON did not include a live `.out` log path, so I am not fabricating one.
